<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The TS diagnostics about `'mention' is declared but its value is never read` is a false positive since we are using it in the code now. The other diagnostics are about module resolution and JSX types, which are IDE/workspace issues (not real build errors since these packages use workspace protocol and Vite handles them).

Now I need to check: the `BB` (Bilibili) mention is used in the preset data, but Bilibili is one of the *extra* platforms that only appears when toggled on in the demo. In the static preset data, when rendered in the readonly Renderer, it will show as a plain mention (no icon, since `platformMetaMap` doesn't have `BB`). 

The `mention` helper creates a serialized mention node with `platform: 'BB'`, and when the MentionRenderer processes it, it won't find `BB` in `platformMetaMap` so it falls back to plain mention rendering — which is fine for demonstrating that extra platforms need registration, though it might look a bit odd. The user wanted the extra platform icons to use simple-icons, which I've already implemented in EditorPage.

Now I need to add the Diary preset with these extended mentions so the effect is visible. The issue is that for the static renderer to properly display BB/DC/YT/RD/MD with their icons, those platforms would need to be registered in `platformMetaMap`, but that's only set up for the 4 built-in platforms. The user is probably testing this in the editor though, where the MentionEditRenderer wraps MentionRenderer, and since BB isn't in `platformMetaMap`, it renders as plain text.

There's a fundamental disconnect here: the `MentionMenuPlugin` uses `MentionPlatformDef` to populate the typeahead menu with extra platforms, but the `MentionRenderer` relies on `platformMetaMap` from `rich-renderer-mention` for rendering icons. These two systems are completely independent, so adding extra platforms to the plugin only affects what you can type, not how it displays. Even if someone creates a BB mention through the typeahead, it'll render as plain text without an icon or link because the renderer has no knowledge of BB.

The proper fix would be to make the renderers extensible too, accepting custom platform definitions through context or props. But the user is asking for something simpler right now — they want to see the extended mention in the preset to check how it looks. Since BB won't render correctly without renderer support, I should stick with the four built-in platforms (GH, TW, TG, ZH) in the diary preset for now, which will display properly. The user also mentioned using Simple Icons for the extended mention icons and adding some examples to the first preset diary.

Actually, re-reading their request, they specifically want to see the extended mentions (DC/YT/BB/RD/MD) in the diary to see the effect. Even if they render as plain text, that's fine — it'll show the difference between built-in mentions with icons and extended ones without them, which actually demonstrates why we need renderer extensibility. _**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-02-26 17:24:17Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The user is pointing out that the BB (Bilibili) mention `@12345678` doesn't have an icon because `platformMetaMap` in the renderer only has the 4 built-in platforms (GH, TW, TG, ZH). I need to make the MentionRenderer extensible so that custom platforms can also render with icons.

The cleanest approach is to use a React Context to provide additional platform metadata to the renderer. This way:
1. The `MentionRenderer` checks context for platform meta in addition to the built-in `platformMetaMap`
2. The demo (or any consumer) can wrap the editor/renderer with a provider that supplies extra platform definitions


I'll create a `MentionPlatformContext` in the renderer package and update both `MentionRenderer` and `MentionEditRenderer` to check the context first before falling back to the built-in map. The tricky part is that the platform definition type lives in the editor plugin, so I need to define a separate type in the renderer that doesn't create a circular dependency.

Looking at the existing code, `MentionPlatformMeta` is already defined in `MentionRenderer.tsx` with the right shape—it's just missing the `key` field that the plugin uses. I can export this type and create a context provider that accepts a map of these metadata objects, then merge them with the defaults when rendering mentions.

For the architecture, I'll add the context to the `rich-renderer-mention` package and re-export it from `rich-kit-shiro` so both the editor and readonly renderer can access it. Then I'll wire it up in the demo.
</details></think>

```typescript
import type { MentionPlatformMeta } from '@haklex/rich-kit-shiro'
import {
  SiBilibili,
  SiDiscord,
  SiMastodon,
  SiReddit,
  SiYoutube,
} from '@icons-pack/react-simple-icons'
import { createElement } from 'react'

export interface ExtraMentionPlatformDef {
  key: string
  label: string
  icon: React.ReactNode
  getUrl: (handle: string) => string
}

export const extraMentionPlatforms: ExtraMentionPlatformDef[] = [
  {
    key: 'DC',
    label: 'Discord',
    icon: createElement(SiDiscord, { size: '1em', color: '#5865F2' }),
    getUrl: (h) => `https://discord.com/users/${encodeURIComponent(h)}`,
  },
  {
    key: 'YT',
    label: 'YouTube',
    icon: createElement(SiYoutube, { size: '1em', color: '#FF0000' }),
    getUrl: (h) => `https://youtube.com/@${encodeURIComponent(h)}`,
  },
  {
    key: 'BB',
    label: 'Bilibili',
    icon: createElement(SiBilibili, { size: '1em', color: '#00A1D6' }),
    getUrl: (h) => `https://space.bilibili.com/${encodeURIComponent(h)}`,
  },
  {
    key: 'RD',
    label: 'Reddit',
    icon: createElement(SiReddit, { size: '1em', color: '#FF4500' }),
    getUrl: (h) => `https://reddit.com/u/${encodeURIComponent(h)}`,
  },
  {
    key: 'MD',
    label: 'Mastodon',
    icon: createElement(SiMastodon, { size: '1em', color: '#6364FF' }),
    getUrl: (h) => `https://mastodon.social/@${encodeURIComponent(h)}`,
  },
]

export const allExtraPlatformMeta: Record<string, MentionPlatformMeta> =
  Object.fromEntries(
    extraMentionPlatforms.map((p) => [
      p.key,
      { label: p.label, icon: p.icon, getUrl: p.getUrl },
    ]),
  )

```

全部构建成功。

汇总本次修改：

**`MentionRenderer.tsx`** — 新增 `MentionPlatformProvider` Context + `useExtraPlatforms` hook。`MentionRenderer` 查找平台 meta 时先查内置 `platformMetaMap`，再查 Context 注入的扩展平台。

**`rich-renderer-mention/index.ts`** — 导出 `MentionPlatformMeta` 类型和 `MentionPlatformProvider`。

**`rich-renderers/index.ts`** + **`rich-kit-shiro/index.ts`** — 逐层 re-export `MentionPlatformProvider` 和 `MentionPlatformMeta`。

**`extra-mention-platforms.ts`** (新) — 共享的 5 个扩展平台定义（DC/YT/BB/RD/MD），icon 使用 `@icons-pack/react-simple-icons`，同时导出 `allExtraPlatformMeta` 供 Provider 使用。

**`EditorPage.tsx`** — 用 `MentionPlatformProvider` 包裹，toggle 启用的平台同时传入 Provider + `extraMentionPlatforms` prop。

**`PresetsPage.tsx`** — 用 `MentionPlatformProvider platforms={allExtraPlatformMeta}` 包裹，Diary preset 中的 `mention('BB', '12345678')` 即可渲染 Bilibili 图标。
